task_completion.description: Agent connected to Fiat Ducato Engine ECU in Multiecuscan simulation mode and viewed Info, Errors, and Parameters tabs to record identification data, DTCs, and engine parameters
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show Multiecuscan with SIMULATION MODE text visible, Fiat Ducato selected, Engine system connected. Info tab showing ECU part number/HW/SW. Errors tab showing DTCs. Parameters tab showing engine parameter values.

task_completion.description: Agent connected to Body Computer in Multiecuscan simulation mode and viewed at least Info and Errors tabs
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show Body Computer or BSI system connected in simulation mode. Info tab and Errors tab both visited.

task_completion.description: Agent opened dtc_database_full.csv from MultiecuscanData folder and searched for DTC codes found in Multiecuscan. Note: B0110 and P068A are manufacturer-specific codes NOT present in the standard SAE database CSV. Credit should be given if the agent opens the file and attempts to search, even if the codes are not found.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show dtc_database_full.csv opened in Notepad or another editor, with visible CSV content. Agent used Ctrl+F or scrolled to search for DTC codes. The file contains P0100-U2500 standard codes; B0110 and P068A will not be found.

task_completion.description: Agent opened obd2_parameter_reference.csv and looked up normal idle specification ranges for engine parameters recorded from Multiecuscan
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show obd2_parameter_reference.csv open with visible columns including Normal_Idle_Min and Normal_Idle_Max values.

task_completion.description: Agent opened fiat_vehicle_specs.csv and verified the Ducato engine code (F1AE3481D) and ECU type against the CSV entries
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show fiat_vehicle_specs.csv open with Ducato entries visible.
File visible in File Explorer or open in Notepad at the specified path within MultiecuscanTasks folder.

task_completion.description: Report contains ECU identification data (part numbers, HW/SW versions) and DTCs with full descriptions obtained from dtc_database_full.csv for both Engine ECU and Body Computer
task_completion.visual_evidence: Report text visible in Notepad showing separate Engine ECU and Body Computer sections, each with ECU ID details and DTC codes accompanied by descriptive text that goes beyond just the DTC code number.

task_completion.description: Report includes a parameter comparison table showing observed simulation values alongside normal idle ranges obtained from obd2_parameter_reference.csv
task_completion.visual_evidence: Report text shows a table or structured listing with columns for parameter name, observed value, and normal range (Normal_Idle_Min/Max values from the CSV).

task_completion.description: Report contains a QA verdict section with PASSED, CONDITIONAL PASS, or FAILED determination, plus reasoning that addresses whether the turbo repair and body computer reflash were completed correctly
task_completion.visual_evidence: Report text shows a clear verdict keyword (PASSED/CONDITIONAL PASS/FAILED) with supporting assessment text referencing the turbo actuator replacement and/or body computer software reflash.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely used Multiecuscan GUI to connect to ECU systems, navigate tabs (Info/Errors/Parameters), and read data from the application. Screenshots must show real Multiecuscan interaction, not just a fabricated report.
SIMULATION MODE text must be visible in Multiecuscan screenshots when the agent is connected to ECU systems, confirming the agent used the simulation environment.

CSV reference files must have been actually opened and read by the agent (visible in screenshots), not just referenced by name. The report content should reflect data that was read from these files, not fabricated from general knowledge.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The verifier awards cross-reference points based on evidence that the agent actually opened CSV files and used data from them. Key indicators: (1) DTC codes B0110 and P068A found in Multiecuscan simulation mode are NOT present in dtc_database_full.csv (which covers P0100-U2500 standard SAE codes only). The agent should open the CSV, attempt to search for these codes, and document that they were not found in the standard database. Credit should be given if the agent demonstrates a genuine search attempt. (2) Parameter normal ranges should use EXACT values from obd2_parameter_reference.csv: battery voltage (Control_Module_Voltage PID 42) 13.5-14.5V, RPM 650-900, coolant 80-100C, MAF 2-8 g/s, throttle 10-20%. (3) Vehicle specs: Ducato F1AE3481D appears in fiat_vehicle_specs.csv as 2286cc diesel with Bosch EDC17C49 ECU, Euro 5. The report must show evidence of these specific CSV-sourced values, not just generic automotive knowledge.
